hypotheses.statement: A pyridine small-molecule inhibitor selectively passivates a-SiN -NH sites, enabling BDEAS-based SiOx growth on a-SiO2 to >=90% selectivity at 10 nm.
hypotheses.rationale: Pyridine acts as a Lewis base that preferentially coordinates to the nitrogen-rich sites of a-SiN, blocking precursor nucleation, while a-SiO2 remains relatively inert to pyridine adsorption, allowing for selective SiOx growth.
hypotheses.novelty_assessment: This leverages specific pyridine-catalyzed inhibition mechanisms on nitride surfaces which have been recently explored for oxide/nitride selectivity.
reasoning_trace: Identify a-SiN as the non-growth surface.
Select pyridine as a known inhibitor for nitride surfaces.
Utilize BDEAS as a standard silicon precursor for SiO2.

supporting_evidence.statement: Pyridine-catalyzed inhibition enhances oxide/nitride selectivity in AS-ALD.

contradicting_evidence.statement: Pyridine may physisorb on SiO2, potentially reducing growth rates on the target surface.

assumptions: Pyridine binding energy to a-SiN is significantly higher than to a-SiO2.
BDEAS precursor does not displace pyridine at the target growth temperature.

hypotheses.statement: A methanesulfonic acid (MSA) inhibitor selectively passivates a-SiN sites, enabling Si(OEt)4-based SiOx growth on a-SiO2 to >=90% selectivity at 10 nm.
hypotheses.rationale: MSA is known to effectively inhibit nucleation on oxide surfaces; by applying it to a-SiN, we hypothesize that the acidic protons interact with the Si-N bonds to form a stable passivating layer that prevents Si(OEt)4 adsorption.
hypotheses.novelty_assessment: Extends the use of MSA from Al2O3 processes to SiO2/SiN systems.
reasoning_trace: MSA is a proven inhibitor for Al2O3.
Acidic inhibitors can passivate surface sites via hydrogen bonding or chemisorption.
Si(OEt)4 is a common precursor for SiO2.

supporting_evidence.statement: Methanesulfonic acid is a successful inhibitor for area-selective ALD of Al2O3.

contradicting_evidence.statement: MSA may etch the a-SiN surface depending on the temperature and moisture content.

assumptions: MSA does not significantly etch the a-SiN surface.
The Si(OEt)4 precursor is sensitive to the MSA blocking layer.
